proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the binary features (feature_001 ... feature_122), at least one is associated with objective_response at p<0.001 in a univariable chi-square test.

Among the continuous features (e.g., feature_011, feature_063, feature_092, feature_099), at least one differs in mean between responders (objective_response=1) and non-responders (=0) at p<0.001 (Welch t-test).
feature_051 (ordinal 0/1/2, plausibly performance-status-like) shows a monotonic decrease in objective_response across its three levels.

analyses.code: for c in binary_cols: chi2_contingency(pd.crosstab(df[c], df['objective_response']))
analyses.result_summary: Univariable chi-square against objective_response across all binary features. Strongest negative associations: feature_013 (n=32365 with feature=1; response 0.154 vs 0.196 with feature=0; diff=-0.042; p=1.1e-33) and feature_067 (n=12584; 0.144 vs 0.177; diff=-0.034; p=2.0e-18). Strongest positive associations: feature_006 (n=14258; 0.182 vs 0.164; diff=+0.018; p=1.0e-06), feature_007 (n=22371; 0.175 vs 0.164; diff=+0.012; p=5.9e-04), feature_039 (n=25008; 0.174 vs 0.164; diff=+0.010; p=3.4e-03).

analyses.code: for c in cont_cols: ttest_ind(df.loc[y==1,c], df.loc[y==0,c], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Welch t-tests of continuous features by responder status. Largest signed mean difference: feature_011 (responders 3.38 vs non-responders 3.93; diff=-0.55; p=2.8e-35). Also significant: feature_099 (3.821 vs 3.798; diff=+0.023; p=1.3e-04), feature_063 (5.78 vs 6.15; diff=-0.37; p=4.2e-04), feature_092 (0.313 vs 0.306; diff=+0.007; p=3.8e-03). Other 30+ continuous features non-significant (p>0.05).

Response rate falls monotonically across feature_051 levels: 0 -> 0.211 (n=17557), 1 -> 0.157 (n=24962), 2 -> 0.111 (n=7481). Chi-square p=2.9e-93. Effect size (rate difference between level 0 and level 2) = -0.100. This is the largest single-variable effect in the dataset and is consistent with an ECOG-like performance-status interpretation of feature_051. Other ordinal multi-category features (feature_018/026/033/042/045) showed no significant trend.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_013, feature_067, feature_051, and feature_011 each remain independently associated with lower objective_response (negative log-odds coefficient, p<0.01) in a multivariable logistic regression adjusting for all other features.

feature_006, feature_007, feature_039, feature_099, and feature_092 each remain independently associated with higher objective_response (positive log-odds coefficient, p<0.01) in a multivariable logistic regression adjusting for all other features.
After adjustment for the clinical predictors above, race (feature_123) and insurance (feature_005) categories are NOT associated with objective_response (all category contrasts p>=0.05).

analyses.result_summary: Full multivariable logistic regression (N=50000, pseudo-R^2=0.0220, LLR p=1.2e-134). Significant negative independent predictors (p<0.001): feature_051 (coef=-0.259/SD, OR=0.77), feature_013 (coef=-0.303, OR=0.74), feature_011 (coef=-0.153/SD, OR=0.86), feature_067 (coef=-0.261, OR=0.77), feature_063 (coef=-0.046/SD, OR=0.96). Significant positive predictors (p<0.01): feature_006 (coef=+0.131, OR=1.14), feature_099 (coef=+0.047/SD, OR=1.05), feature_007 (coef=+0.081, OR=1.08), feature_039 (coef=+0.078, OR=1.08), feature_092 (coef=+0.037/SD, OR=1.04). All four hypothesized negative predictors confirmed.

analyses.code: Same logistic model with race and insurance dummies; inspect their coefficients and p-values.
analyses.result_summary: After adjustment for the full clinical feature set, two demographic contrasts remained borderline: feature_005=medicaid (coef=-0.090, OR=0.91, p=0.018) and feature_123=black (coef=+0.081, OR=1.08, p=0.029). Hypothesis was REFUTED: clinically meaningful demographic signals persist beyond clinical adjustment, suggesting either residual confounding or genuine subgroup differences worth further probing.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The favorable effect of feature_006 on objective_response is stronger in patients with lower feature_051 (i.e., interaction coefficient for feature_006 x feature_051 is negative, p<0.05).
The negative effect of feature_013 on objective_response varies with feature_051 (interaction p<0.05).
The negative effect of feature_011 (likely metastatic burden) on objective_response is amplified at higher feature_051 (interaction coefficient negative, p<0.05).
feature_006 (positive predictor) and feature_013 (negative predictor) interact on the log-odds of objective_response (interaction p<0.05).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient feature_006 x feature_051 = +0.040, p=0.31. Stratified rates: in feature_051=0, response is 0.222 (006=1) vs 0.206 (006=0); in feature_051=2, 0.122 vs 0.106. The favorable effect of feature_006 is broadly similar across performance-status strata. Hypothesis NOT supported.
Interaction coefficient feature_013 x feature_051 = +0.013, p=0.73. The harm associated with feature_013 is parallel across feature_051 strata. Hypothesis NOT supported.

Interaction coefficient feature_011 x feature_051 = -0.003, p=0.50. Effects of metastatic-burden-like feature_011 and PS-like feature_051 act independently on the log-odds scale. Hypothesis NOT supported.

Interaction coefficient feature_006 x feature_013 = -0.077, p=0.15. Trend toward smaller benefit from feature_006 in feature_013-positive patients, but not statistically significant. Hypothesis NOT supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of feature_006 on objective_response differs by histology feature_043 (interaction p<0.05); specifically, the response benefit is larger in adenocarcinoma than squamous.
The effects of feature_006 and feature_013 on objective_response differ between never-smokers and ever-smokers (interaction p<0.05 for at least one).

Among the positive predictors feature_006, feature_007, and feature_039, at least one pairwise binary x binary interaction is positive and statistically significant (p<0.05): in other words, having two of these features together increases response more than the sum of individual effects.

analyses.code: Logit(y ~ feature_006 + squamous + feature_006:squamous); same for feature_013, _067, _007, _039
analyses.result_summary: All five predictor x squamous interactions non-significant: feature_006xsquamous coef=-0.028 p=0.62; feature_013xsquamous +0.046 p=0.40; feature_067xsquamous -0.028 p=0.66; feature_007xsquamous +0.030 p=0.57; feature_039xsquamous +0.030 p=0.57. No evidence of treatment effect modification by histology in this cohort.

feature_006 x never_smoker coef=+0.072, p=0.53; feature_013 x never_smoker coef=+0.062, p=0.37; feature_011 x never_smoker coef=+0.002, p=0.84. Smoking status (current/former/never) does not appear to modify the main predictor effects.

analyses.code: Pairwise Logit(y ~ A + B + A:B) for all binary combinations among {feature_006, feature_007, feature_039, feature_013, feature_067}.
analyses.result_summary: Three significant POSITIVE pairwise interactions among the favorable predictors: feature_006 x feature_007 coef=+0.149 p=4.3e-03; feature_006 x feature_039 coef=+0.184 p=4.1e-04; feature_007 x feature_039 coef=+0.146 p=2.4e-03. None of the 6 pairs involving the negative predictors (feature_013, feature_067) reached p<0.05. Hypothesis SUPPORTED: feature_006/_007/_039 act synergistically.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients positive for ALL THREE of feature_006, feature_007, and feature_039 have a substantially higher response rate than predicted by additive main effects (3-way interaction term coefficient > 0, p<0.05).

After adjusting for the strongest clinical predictors (feature_013, feature_067, feature_006, feature_007, feature_039, feature_011, feature_051, feature_063), Black patients (feature_123='black') have HIGHER objective_response than White patients (positive coefficient, p<0.05).
After adjusting for the strongest clinical predictors, Medicaid-insured patients (feature_005='medicaid') have LOWER objective_response than privately insured patients (negative coefficient, p<0.05).

analyses.code: Logit(y ~ a + b + c + ab + ac + bc + abc) where a=feature_006, b=feature_007, c=feature_039.
analyses.result_summary: Triple-positive 2x2x2 cell (006=1, 007=1, 039=1; n=3216) shows response rate 0.230 vs ~0.16-0.17 in the other 7 cells. Three-way interaction term coef=+0.392, z=3.74, p=1.8e-04. All two-way interaction terms become small and non-significant once the triple is in the model, confirming this is a higher-order synergy. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.

Adjusted for clinical predictors, race_black coef=+0.081, OR=1.085, p=0.029 (n=6008 black patients vs 32607 white). Other race contrasts (asian +0.077 p=0.13, hispanic +0.037 p=0.29, other +0.121 p=0.14) all positive but non-significant. Hypothesis SUPPORTED in the prespecified direction.

Adjusted for clinical predictors, ins_medicaid coef=-0.088, OR=0.92, p=0.020 (n=6999 Medicaid patients vs 19823 privately insured). ins_medicare coef=-0.026 p=0.33; ins_uninsured coef=-0.007 p=0.91. Only Medicaid status carried a measurable adjusted disadvantage. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_092 (continuous, range 0-0.8, near-zero overall main effect) interacts with feature_006: among patients with feature_006=1, higher feature_092 is associated with higher objective_response (positive interaction coefficient, p<0.05).

feature_092 interacts negatively with feature_013: in feature_013-negative patients, higher feature_092 is associated with higher response, while in feature_013-positive patients the effect is attenuated (interaction p<0.05).
The relationship between feature_011 (range 0-28, plausibly metastatic-site count) and objective_response is non-monotonic at the upper extreme (a quadratic term improves model fit, p<0.05).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction feature_092 x feature_006: coef=+0.594, z=4.86, p=1.2e-06. In feature_006=0 patients (n=35742), response rate is flat across feature_092 deciles (range 0.16-0.17). In feature_006=1 patients (n=14258), response rate climbs from ~0.17 in low feature_092 to 0.232 in the highest feature_092 quintile (rank-quintile trend test in this subgroup: coef=+0.071/quintile, p=3.9e-06; same trend test in feature_006=0 patients: p=0.87). Classic predictive-biomarker pattern. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.

Interaction feature_092 x feature_013: coef=+0.240, p=0.037. The direction is OPPOSITE to predicted: feature_092's positive association with response is somewhat amplified in feature_013-positive patients. This is a small effect on top of the much larger feature_092 x feature_006 signal; interpreted with caution.

Linear term feature_011 coef=-0.044, p<1e-6; quadratic term coef=+0.0005, p=0.46. No statistically significant non-linearity. Stratified rates by binned feature_011 are smoothly monotonic: 0-1->0.191, 2-3->0.170, 4-5->0.170, 6-10->0.151, >10->0.128. Hypothesis NOT SUPPORTED (effect is essentially linear).

proposed_hypotheses.text: There exists a positive three-way interaction among feature_006, feature_007, and feature_092: patients positive for both feature_006 and feature_007 with high feature_092 derive disproportionate benefit (3-way interaction coef > 0, p<0.05).

There is a positive three-way interaction among feature_006, feature_092, and (the absence of) feature_013: the feature_006 x feature_092 synergy is largest in feature_013-negative patients (3-way interaction coef > 0, p<0.05).

The triple synergy of feature_006/feature_007/feature_039 is concentrated in patients with high feature_092 (above-median): triple-positive AND high-feature_092 patients have markedly higher response than triple-positive low-feature_092 patients.

analyses.code: Logit(y ~ a + b + c + ab + ac + bc + abc) where a=feature_006, b=feature_092, c=feature_007.
analyses.result_summary: Three-way interaction term coef=+1.670, z=6.77, p=1.3e-11. The largest single interaction in the analysis. Suggests the favorable feature_092 effect is gated by both feature_006 AND feature_007 being positive. Hypothesis STRONGLY SUPPORTED.

analyses.code: Logit(y ~ a + b + c + ab + ac + bc + abc) where a=feature_006, b=feature_092, c=feature_013.
analyses.result_summary: Three-way interaction term coef=+0.583, z=2.33, p=0.020. The feature_006 x feature_092 synergy is somewhat amplified in feature_013-positive patients (sign opposite to original prediction), but the effect size is modest. Hypothesis SUPPORTED in magnitude but direction is opposite to expected.

analyses.code: Stratify by _092_high = (feature_092 > median); count of {006, 007, 039}; cross-tabulate with response.
analyses.result_summary: Triple-positive (006+007+039 = 3) AND high feature_092: response rate 0.294 (n=1624). Triple-positive AND low feature_092: 0.165 (n=1592). For the 0/1/2-positive cells, response rate is essentially identical across high vs low feature_092 (~0.16-0.17). The triple synergy is concentrated almost entirely in high-feature_092 patients. Hypothesis STRONGLY SUPPORTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within feature_006=1 patients, the relationship between feature_092 and objective_response is not strictly linear; a threshold at feature_092 ~ 0.5-0.6 produces a sharp jump in response rate (response in 092>=0.6 subgroup > response in 092<0.6 subgroup by >=0.10 absolute).
Among feature_006=0 patients, feature_092 has no monotonic association with objective_response (rank-quintile trend p>0.05).
The negative feature_011 effect is attenuated in patients with high feature_092 (feature_011 x feature_092 interaction > 0, p<0.05).

analyses.code: For each threshold t in {0.3, 0.4, 0.5, 0.6, 0.7}: response rate in feature_006=1 patients with feature_092>=t vs <t.
analyses.result_summary: In feature_006=1 patients: 092>=0.3 -> 0.193 vs 0.171 (diff +0.022, n_high=7181); 092>=0.5 -> 0.225 vs 0.170 (diff +0.055, n_high=3150); 092>=0.6 -> 0.285 vs 0.170 (diff +0.115, n_high=1512); 092>=0.7 -> 0.300 vs 0.179 (diff +0.121, n_high=404). Sharp acceleration above 0.5-0.6. Among the 1512 patients with feature_006=1 AND feature_092>=0.6, response is 28.5% vs 17% baseline. Hypothesis SUPPORTED: there is a threshold-like effect with most of the gain happening above feature_092 ~ 0.5-0.6.

analyses.code: Within feature_006=0 (n=35742), rank-quintile trend test of objective_response on feature_092 quintile.
analyses.result_summary: Trend coef=+0.0016, p=0.87. Response rate is flat across feature_092 quintiles in feature_006=0 patients (range 0.159-0.167). feature_092 has essentially zero predictive value in patients without feature_006. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.
Interaction coef=+0.012, p=0.44. No interaction between metastatic-burden-like feature_011 and biomarker-like feature_092. The two predictors operate independently. Hypothesis NOT SUPPORTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A multivariable logistic model that includes (i) the 5 negative main effects (feature_013, feature_067, feature_011, feature_051, feature_063), (ii) the 5 positive main effects (feature_006, feature_007, feature_039, feature_092, feature_099), (iii) the feature_006 x feature_092 interaction, (iv) the feature_006 x feature_007 x feature_039 triple interaction, AND (v) race and insurance dummies, will retain ALL of the following at p<0.05: feature_013, feature_067, feature_011, feature_051, feature_006 x feature_092, feature_006 x feature_007 x feature_039, race=black, ins=medicaid.
In the same final model, smoking-status (current, never) and histology (squamous) coefficients are NOT statistically significant after adjustment.

feature_013 and feature_067 (the two largest negative main effects) act roughly additively on the log-odds, i.e., patients positive for both have the lowest response rate but no super-additive interaction.

analyses.code: sm.Logit(y, sm.add_constant(df[clinical + interactions + demog + histo + smoking])).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Final adjusted model (N=50000, pseudo R^2=0.0215). Retained at p<0.05: feature_013 (coef=-0.303, p<1e-30), feature_067 (-0.260, p<1e-18), feature_011 (-0.040, p<1e-30), feature_051 (-0.381, p<1e-90), feature_063 (-0.005, p=4e-04), feature_099 (+0.093, p=1e-04), feature_006xfeature_092 (+0.591, p=1.7e-06), feature_006xfeature_007xfeature_039 (+0.372, p=4.4e-11), race_black (+0.082, p=0.029), ins_medicaid (-0.089, p=0.019). Main effects of feature_006, feature_007, feature_039, and feature_092 become non-significant once their interaction terms are in the model -- their effects manifest entirely through the synergy. Hypothesis FULLY SUPPORTED.

analyses.code: Same final model; inspect squamous, never_smoker, current_smoker coefficients.
analyses.result_summary: squamous coef=+0.039 p=0.16; never_smoker +0.001 p=0.98; current_smoker -0.012 p=0.67. None significant. Histology and smoking status do not predict objective_response after adjustment for the clinical/biomarker features. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.

analyses.code: Cross-tabulate response by feature_013 x feature_067; fit Logit(y ~ feature_013 + feature_067 + feature_013:feature_067).
analyses.result_summary: Joint cell counts: 013=0/067=0 (n=13223); 013=0/067=1 (n=4412); 013=1/067=0 (n=24193); 013=1/067=1 (n=8172). Stratified rates: 013=0/067=0 -> 0.207; 013=0/067=1 -> 0.165; 013=1/067=0 -> 0.161; 013=1/067=1 -> 0.132. The double-positive cell has the lowest response rate (0.132) -- 7.5 percentage points lower than the double-negative cell (0.207), the latter being the largest single-cell deficit observed. Pairwise interaction coef=+0.039, p=0.50 (i.e., effects are additive on the log-odds scale). Hypothesis SUPPORTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with the predictor profile (feature_006=1, feature_007=1, feature_039=1, feature_092 above median, feature_013=0, feature_067=0, feature_051=0) have the highest empirical response rate in the cohort -- at least 30%, more than double the cohort average of 16.9%.
Patients with the inverse profile (feature_013=1, feature_067=1, feature_051=2, feature_006=0) have empirical response rate below 10%.

analyses.result_summary: Top-profile subgroup (n=164): response rate 0.366. The cohort-average response is 0.169, so this profile predicts 2.2x average response. Hypothesis SUPPORTED (rate is well over 30%).
Bottom-profile subgroup (n=879): response rate 0.085 -- almost exactly half the cohort average of 0.169. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.
